mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of real personal savings (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates a synchronized pullback in consumer and business demand, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns when both series decline simultaneously, or the interaction term shows a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of constrained industrial production growth, supply bottlenecks elevate input costs and profit margins, causing the S&P 500 to underperform over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions turn negative.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO growth decelerates below 1.0% annualized, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) serves as a leading indicator of aggregate corporate revenue trends, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days when GDP growth exceeds the long-term trend rate.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of observations when GDP growth is above trend, or the rolling 1-year Spearman correlation with SPX returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5 percent, the cost of capital exceeds the historical average corporate return on invested capital, forcing equity multiples to contract and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity valuations expand or remain stable when FEDFUNDS breaches the 4.5 percent threshold, or the signal's hit rate for negative returns falls below 40% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous acceleration of nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and deceleration of the unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirms a self-reinforcing labor demand expansion, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal predicts positive returns but SPX delivers negative or flat performance over the 60-day window, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary environment where CPI growth falls below 2.5 percent annually, the Federal Reserve's policy flexibility supports risk assets, causing the S&P 500 to outperform over the next 5 trading days as real yields decline.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL growth decelerates below 2.5 percent, or the forward return differential between low-inflation and high-inflation regimes is statistically indistinguishable from zero.
